thinking.text:
Let me analyze what I have so far. I've extracted OCR text from 190 frames (at 1fps) of a 3-minute-10-second Zork gameplay video. The OCR has many errors. I need to build a correct solution file with all the player's commands.

Looking at the OCR outputs, I can piece together the commands. The transcript shows commands after '>' prompts. I need to extract all actual commands the player typed.

Let me take a more systematic approach - I'll examine all frames and manually compile the correct command list based on what the OCR shows, fixing obvious OCR errors.

From what I've seen so far, the commands include:
- n, n, u (early game - going north then up a tree)
- get (get the egg)
- d, s, e, open, w, get all, move rug, open case, open trap, light lamp
- s, e, get, w, n, get, w, n, n, slay troll, g (get), g (get?)
- w, s, e, s, e, u, get bag, s
- e, s, se, ulysse (say ulysses), e, e, put all, put all but lamp, case, w
- w, temple, d, open solid, get sharp, d, ... etc.

But the OCR is noisy. Let me try a different approach - I'll look at the HQ frames (5fps) which have more temporal resolution, and see if I can get better OCR results by using specific frames where the command is fully visible.

Actually, looking at the earlier outputs, I had some success with direct tesseract calls on specific frames. Let me examine the full text of several OCR files to understand what commands were actually executed, then build a clean list.

Let me examine several OCR output files to see the full game transcript and extract the actual commands.
